other_observers: Larry Semo; I found and identified the bird.
specific_location: at Box Springs Pond on the east side of Hwy 71 and the south side of BB Road along the Horse Creek drainage
description: A largish empie, the bird was brown above with a strong olive cast. The eye ring was very narrow, yellowish, and almost complete (possibly broken on top). The whitish throat contrasted quite sharply with the darker auriculars. The chest had a grayish-olive wash separating the white throat from the very pale yellow belly. The wings were blackish with strong orange-buffy wingbars. The primary projection was fairly long, being equal in length to something more than half the length of the longest tertial. The tail was dark with paler outer webs to the R6s. The bill was fairly wide with a black maxilla and with the mandible mostly yellowish-orange; about the distal 20% was dark. The legs were dark.Behaviors: foraging among cottonwoods and rushesCall: I heard the bird twice give the typical lower-pitched "peip" callPlumage: Juvenal
photographer: Photographer: Tony Leukering, Tony Leukering, T
